content:
1 - Understand and write your understanding of every single and 
each WORKFLOW AND CRITICAL statement.
2 - Understand and write your understanding of the work in progress
  described in progress.txt and compared with the actual files.

WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, plan next improvements, add tasks and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   - Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   - Execute task
   - Run 'bun run check' and fix found issues.     
   - Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   - Review output and consider improvements
   - Implement corrections if needed
   - Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   - Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   - Output status block (see below)
   - Stop

CRITICAL:
- ALL tasks and instructions are deliberate. 
- No task is optional.
- If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
- Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
- Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)
- No task should be submitted as "look reasonable." 
- Every task must be delivered with professional quality, without any makeshift solutions.
- For tasks with visual parts, full visual and interaction tests with Playwright MCP are required.
- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.
- Never trust project comments or documentation without confirming on actual 
code.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

content.text:
## Understanding of WORKFLOW Statements

1. **Understand Project** – Read and internalize the project structure, tech stack, and conventions before doing any work.
2. **Read progress.txt and identify the most important pending task** – Check `.claude/progress.txt` for tasks. Prioritize by `[P<number>]` tags: `[P0]` is highest priority (must be done first). Tasks with priority >999 (e.g. `[P1000]`) are low-priority — do them when possible but don't block on them if higher-priority work exists.
3. **If NO pending tasks exist** – Plan next improvements, add tasks to progress.txt, and stop.
4. **If tasks exist** – Mark the chosen task as IN_PROGRESS, then sequentially: evaluate scope (break into sub-tasks if >3 files), execute, run `bun run check`, launch a review agent for PR-style review, consider and implement corrections, update progress.txt, commit, output status block, and stop.

## Understanding of CRITICAL Statements

- Every task is intentional and mandatory — nothing is optional.
- If more work is needed beyond current scope, add a new task and set `"exit": false`.
- Never delete tasks from progress.txt (only update their status).
- No workarounds allowed (e.g. `as any` is unacceptable) — fix root causes.
- Professional quality is required — no "looks reasonable" submissions.
- Visual tasks require Playwright MCP visual/interaction tests.
- No errors or mistakes are acceptable.
- Never trust comments/docs — verify against actual code.

Now let me read progress.txt and understand the current state.

     - [x] L03590e Viewport responsiveness: max-width: calc(100vw - 48px) on

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

     drills into a command, show a breadcrumb trail in the search bar area
     (e.g. "Raycast > Clipboard History"). Breadcrumb appears as a small

     that appears at bottom-center of the command palette. Supports styles:
     success (green), error (red), info (default). Shows icon, title, and
     optional message. Auto-dismisses after 3s or on click. Animated

     (CSS Grid). Each GridItem shows an image/icon (larger, ~64x64),

- [x] UXDS001 - The design is very different from Raycast, colors,
  spacing, icons, etc... fix it.
  Sub-tasks:

    filled icons matching real app branding (Safari, VS Code, Terminal, Figma,

    filled icons for Raycast commands (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Window
    Management, Calculator, Dictionary, System Monitor, Color Picker).

    a colored gradient rounded-rect background with white icon symbols inside.

    theme (refine background, surface, border, text colors; adjust opacity
    and blur values on command palette window). Updated: background #191919,

    12px bottom radius, command palette radius 16→12px, list padding 0 8px,
    section headers 12px font, kbd 20x20 with 12px font, search icon 20x20.
    Added $font-base, $list-item-height, $action-panel-height tokens.

- [x] UXDS002 - The design is very poor, Raycast has a lot more resources and
screens, plan next tasks and fix it.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] UXDS002a - [P0] Add Inter font and update font stack. Raycast uses Inter
    as primary font with custom letter/line spacing. Add Inter via Google Fonts
    or local @font-face. Update $font-family in _tokens.scss to prioritize Inter.
    Done: Inter prioritized in $font-family, letter-spacing -0.011em on body,
    added $font-weight-regular (400) and $font-weight-medium (500) tokens.
    Inter was already loaded in index.html (weights 400, 500, 600).
  - [x] UXDS002b - [P0] Fix selection/active item style. Raycast uses selection
    color at 10% alpha for active items (subtle highlight), NOT full accent blue.
    Update list_item.scss: active bg from $color-accent to rgba($color-accent, 0.1).
    Active title/subtitle/accessory text stays primary color (no white override).
    Add new token $color-selection-background: rgba($color-accent, 0.1).
  - [x] UXDS002c - [P0] Fix window dimensions and glass effect. Window radius 12→18px
    (add $radius-window: 18px token). Search bar height 46→56px. Search bar font
    15→16px ($font-search: 16px). Backdrop blur 64→72px. Background: linear-gradient
    from backgroundPrimary(40%) to backgroundSecondary(40%). Border: inset box-shadow
    1px with text at 20% opacity. Action panel bottom radius uses $radius-window.
  - [x] UXDS002d - [P1] Fix list item spacing and radius. Border-radius 8→10px
    (add $radius-list-item: 10px token). Icon-text gap 8→12px. Left padding 8px
    stays. These match Raycast's ray-so source exactly.
  - [x] UXDS002e - [P1] Fix section headers. Font-weight to 500 (medium, add
    $font-weight-medium: 500 token). Letter-spacing from 0.5px to 0.1px. Text
    color from $color-text-secondary to rgba($color-text-primary, 0.6) for
    proper alpha-based approach. Remove text-transform uppercase (Raycast section
    headers are mixed case).
  - [x] UXDS002f - [P1] Fix action bar layout. Left side: add Raycast logo icon
    (small 16x16 SVG) + context label (current extension/view name) in $font-sm
    at 60% text opacity. Right side: primary action label in $font-xs font-semibold
    full text color + Enter kbd badge + vertical separator (2x12px at text/10%) +
    "Actions" label at 60% + Cmd+K badges. Background from $color-surface-footer
    to rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05).
  - [x] UXDS002g - [P1] Fix kbd badge styling. Background from $color-kbd-background
    to rgba($color-text-primary, 0.1). Remove 1px border. Border-radius from 4px
    to 6px ($radius-md or new $radius-kbd: 6px). Size stays 20px height but Raycast
    uses 22x20px for single chars, 24x20px for symbols.
  - [x] UXDS002h - [P2] Fix loading bar to match Raycast. Height 2→1px. Gradient
    width constrained to 200px (not full width). Animation duration 1.5→2s. Gradient:
    transparent→loader color→transparent (simpler 3-stop gradient). Added
    $command-palette-width token. Animation only runs when visible. Linear timing.
  - [x] UXDS002i - [P2] Adopt alpha-based color token system. Raycast derives all
    UI colors from base text color at various opacities (10%, 40%, 60%). Replace
    flat colors ($color-text-secondary, $color-text-tertiary, $color-border) with
    alpha variants of $color-text-primary. Add tokens: $text-100 (10%), $text-400
    (40%), $text-600 (60%), $border-100 (text 10%), $border-200 (text 20%).
  - [x] UXDS002j - [P2] Add colored accessory tags on list items. Raycast shows
    colored tag pills in list item accessories (22px tall, rounded-md 6px, color
    text on 15% alpha bg). Update ListItemAccessoryData type to support tag
    accessories with color. Create accessory tag styles in list_item.scss.

  feature pages, Tauri v2 capabilities). Planned 55 new tasks across 7
  categories: Tauri Desktop Integration (10), Missing Raycast Screens (6),
  Missing UI Components (9), Visual Polish (7), Search & Interaction (6),
  Infrastructure (9), Testing (8). Total pending tasks: 66 (11 existing + 55
  new). Tasks prioritized P0-P3 based on Raycast core feature importance and
  dependencies.

file.content:
=== CORE MISSING COMPONENTS ===

- [x] [P0] COMP001 - Build Alert/Confirm dialog component. Blocking modal overlay
  for destructive or important confirmations. Fields: title, message, icon,
  primaryAction (label + style: Default/Destructive/Cancel), dismissAction.
  Animate in with backdrop blur + scale. Create src/components/alert/ with
  alert.tsx and alert.scss. Create useAlert hook in src/hooks/use_alert.ts that
  returns confirmAlert(options) -> Promise<boolean>. Wire demo: add "Delete"
  action to list items that triggers confirm dialog before toast.
  Done: Alert component with backdrop blur overlay, scale+fade enter/exit
  animations, focus trap (Tab cycling), Escape to dismiss, backdrop click to
  dismiss. useAlert hook with Promise-based confirmAlert() using ref-based
  resolve (no double-resolve). All SCSS values tokenized ($alert-dialog-width,
  $backdrop-blur, $backdrop-overlay added to _tokens.scss). Unique IDs via
  useId(). Stable callback refs to avoid stale closures. Delete action in
  dropdown triggers destructive confirm dialog before showing toast.

- [x] [P0] COMP002 - Build proper navigation stack system (push/pop). Replace
  current breadcrumb-only drill-in with a real stack-based navigation. Create
  src/hooks/use_navigation.ts with push(component), pop(), popToRoot(). Each
  pushed view is a full-screen replacement inside CommandPalette. Escape pops
  the stack. Breadcrumb updates automatically from stack. Animate transitions
  (slide-left on push, slide-right on pop). Update App.tsx to use navigation
  stack instead of viewMode state. Action.Push should be declarative.
  Done: useNavigationStack hook with push/pop/popToRoot, monotonic counter for
  stable animation keys, functional updater guards for race-safe pop/popToRoot,
  shared BreadcrumbItem type between hook and SearchBar, extracted ColorPickerView
  and DetailView as standalone components, CSS slide transitions in
  command_palette.scss, SearchBar Escape-to-pop with query-clear-first behavior.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] COMP002a - Create use_navigation hook (src/hooks/use_navigation.ts) with
    NavigationStack type, push(title, component), pop(), popToRoot(). Derive
    breadcrumbs automatically from stack. Export NavigationProvider context.
  - [x] COMP002b - Add navigation transition styles to command_palette.scss.
    Slide-left on push, slide-right on pop. CSS-only transitions.
  - [x] COMP002c - Refactor App.tsx to use navigation stack. Replace viewMode/
    drilledItem/breadcrumbs state with useNavigation hook. Extract ColorPickerView
    and CreateSnippetView as standalone pushed components. Root view stays inline.
  - [x] COMP002d - Update SearchBar to consume breadcrumbs from navigation
    context (or keep prop-based) and wire Escape to pop().

- [x] [P0] COMP003 - Add ActionPanel.Section support. Group actions in the
  dropdown with visual section separators and optional section titles. Update
  actions_dropdown.tsx to accept sections (array of {title?, actions[]}). Render
  separator line + small title between groups. Update App.tsx demo to show
  sections: "Primary Actions", "Copy Actions", "Danger Zone".
  Done: DropdownSection type with optional title. Sections prop replaces flat
  actions. Separators between groups (outside role="group"). Empty sections
  filtered. aria-activedescendant on menu, aria-labelledby on titled groups.
  Precomputed sectionOffsets. Index-based keys. App.tsx demo with 3 sections.
  26 dropdown tests (5 new), all 74 tests pass.

- [x] [P0] COMP004 - Add ActionPanel.Submenu support. Nested submenu that
  replaces the dropdown content with child actions when hovered/selected. Show
  chevron-right indicator on parent item. Escape goes back to parent menu.
  Animate sideways transition. Update actions_dropdown.tsx.
  Done: DropdownAction.submenu field with DropdownSection[]. SubmenuState tracks
  parentActiveIndex for restore on exit. ChevronRight indicator on submenu items.
  Back button with role="menuitem" and aria-label. Slide-left/slide-right CSS
  animations. ArrowRight/Enter to enter, ArrowLeft/Escape to go back (restores
  parent position). App.tsx demo with "Copy As..." submenu (3 child actions).
  45 dropdown tests (5 new for submenu), all 93 tests pass.

- [x] [P0] COMP005 - Build List.Dropdown component (search bar filter). A small
  dropdown selector inside the SearchBar (right side) for secondary filtering
  (e.g., filter by type: All, Commands, Applications). Create
  src/components/search_bar/search_dropdown.tsx and search_dropdown.scss.
  Dropdown appears below search bar on click, styled like actions_dropdown.
  Supports sections. Update SearchBar to accept optional dropdown prop.
  Done: SearchDropdown component with sections support, keyboard navigation
  (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Escape, Tab closes), click-outside dismiss, check
  icon for selected option, chevron rotation animation. Proper ARIA: listbox
  role, option role on divs, aria-selected, aria-activedescendant, useId()
  for unique IDs, aria-label on trigger. All values tokenized
  ($search-dropdown-trigger-height, $search-dropdown-menu-min-width,
  $search-dropdown-menu-max-height, $z-dropdown, $z-search-dropdown).
  SearchBar accepts optional dropdown ReactNode prop. App.tsx demo with
  All/Commands/Applications filter. 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP006 - Build HUD notification component. Compact floating pill
  shown at bottom-center of screen after an action completes (like "Copied to
  clipboard"). Auto-dismisses after 1.5s. Simpler than toast: just icon + text.
  Animated entrance (scale-up + fade). Create src/components/hud/ with hud.tsx
  and hud.scss. Create useHUD hook in src/hooks/use_hud.ts. Wire demo: "Copy"
  action shows HUD.
  Done: HUD component with pill shape ($radius-pill token), scale+fade
  enter/exit animations, role="status" aria-live="polite". useHUD hook follows
  useToast pattern (timer management, exit animation, auto-dismiss 1.5s).
  HUDContainer absolute-positioned at bottom-center with pointer-events:none.
  $z-hud: 120 token. Copy Name, Copy As... submenu, and grid color copy actions
  all use HUD instead of toast. CheckIcon and ClipboardHUDIcon inline SVGs.
  max-width: 280px with text-overflow ellipsis. All 93 tests pass.

- [x] [P1] COMP007 - Build Form.PasswordField component. Masked text input with
  optional reveal toggle button (eye icon). Same styling as FormTextField but
  type="password". Create src/components/form/form_password_field.tsx. Add to
  form index.ts exports.
  Done: FormPasswordField component with password/text toggle via eye icon button.
  Follows FormTextField pattern exactly (useId, FormField wrapper, aria-invalid,
  aria-describedby). Reveal toggle: useState for revealed state, type switches
  between "password" and "text". EyeIcon/EyeOffIcon inline SVGs (16x16, 1.25
  stroke). Toggle button: keyboard-accessible with focus-visible outline,
  aria-label updates dynamically. SCSS: form-password-wrapper (relative flex),
  form-input--password (right padding for toggle), form-password-toggle (absolute
  positioned, $space-6 size, $text-400 color with hover to primary). All values
  tokenized. autoComplete="current-password" for proper browser/password manager
  behavior. Exported from form/index.ts.

- [x] [P1] COMP008 - Build Form.TagPicker component. Multi-select input where
  user picks tags from a dropdown. Selected tags appear as pills inside the
  input. Type to filter available tags. Remove tag by clicking X or backspace.
  Create src/components/form/form_tag_picker.tsx and form_tag_picker.scss.
  Done: FormTagPicker component with combobox ARIA pattern, filtered dropdown,
  tag pills with X remove button, Backspace removes last tag, ArrowDown/Up
  keyboard navigation, Enter to select, Escape/Tab to close. useMemo for
  availableOptions and optionLabelMap for O(1) label lookups. Value-based
  option IDs for stable aria-activedescendant. All SCSS values tokenized
  (local vars for tag height, remove size, input min-width, menu max-height).
  FormField wrapper, useId(), aria-invalid/aria-describedby for errors,
  aria-label on listbox. Exported from form/index.ts with FormTagPickerOption
  type. Demo: Tags field in Create Snippet form with 8 language options.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP009 - Build Form.FilePicker component (simulated for web). Shows
  a file input styled like Raycast's file picker. Single or multiple file
  selection. Shows selected file name(s) as pills. Create
  src/components/form/form_file_picker.tsx and form_file_picker.scss.
  Done: FormFilePicker component with hidden native file input, browse button,
  selected file pills (name + size + remove button). Supports single/multiple
  file selection, accept filter, file deduplication by name+size+type in multi
  mode. SCSS: BEM naming, all values tokenized (local vars for pill height,
  max-width, remove size). ARIA: role="list"/role="listitem" on file pills,
  aria-label on remove buttons, aria-invalid/aria-describedby for errors.
  FilePickerFile type and FormFilePicker exported from form/index.ts.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP010 - Build Form.Separator component. Visual horizontal divider
  line within forms. Thin line (1px, $border-100 color) with optional margin.
  Create in src/components/form/form_separator.tsx. Simple component.
  Done: FormSeparator component using semantic <hr> element with implicit
  separator role. SCSS: reset browser defaults (border:none, margin:0, padding:0,
  height:0), border-top 1px solid $border-100. No props (consistent with other
  form components). Exported from form/index.ts. All checks pass.

- [x] [P1] COMP011 - Add date accessory type to ListItem. List item accessories
  should support {date: Date} which renders as relative time ("2 hours ago",
  "Yesterday", "Mar 1"). Update ListItemAccessoryData type in types.ts. Add
  date formatting utility in src/utils/format_date.ts. Update list_item.tsx
  rendering.
  Done: Added `date?: Date` field to `ListItemAccessoryData` in types.ts.
  Created `src/utils/format_date.ts` with `formatRelativeDate()` using
  calendar-day-based logic for Yesterday/N days ago (not elapsed-time-based).
  Ranges: Just now (<1min), N minutes ago (<1hr), N hours ago (same calendar
  day), Yesterday (1 calendar day), N days ago (2-6 calendar days), absolute
  date (7+ days or different year). ListItem renders date accessories with
  formatted relative text and `title` attribute showing full localized date on
  hover. Consistent `now` reference via `useMemo` prevents label inconsistency
  within a single render pass. Added date accessories to 3 mock items
  (Clipboard History: 30min, Snippets: 3hr, Window Management: 2 days).
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P1] COMP012 - Add tooltip support to accessories and list items. On hover
  over an accessory or list item, show a small tooltip with additional info.
  Create src/components/tooltip/ with tooltip.tsx and tooltip.scss. Position
  below/above element. Fade-in after 500ms delay. Update ListItem to use.
  Done: Tooltip component with 500ms hover delay, auto-flip positioning
  (below by default, above when near viewport bottom), fade-in animation with
  correct direction per position, BEM SCSS (tooltip/tooltip__bubble/--above),
  all values tokenized ($z-tooltip: 90, $tooltip-max-width: 240px local var).
  ARIA: role="tooltip", useId() for aria-describedby pairing. Timer cleanup
  on unmount. ListItem updated: AccessoryWrapper conditionally wraps accessories
  with Tooltip when tooltip text exists. Date accessories auto-get full date
  tooltip. Explicit tooltip field overrides via nullish coalescing. Added
  tooltip?: string field to ListItemAccessoryData type. Demo tooltip on
  Clipboard History "Raycast" accessory. All 93 tests pass.

- [x] [P2] COMP013 - Build Quick Look preview overlay. Full-screen-ish preview
  overlay triggered by spacebar or Action.ToggleQuickLook. Shows larger preview
  of selected item's detail content. Dark backdrop + centered card. Close on
  Escape or spacebar again. Create src/components/quick_look/ with
  quick_look.tsx and quick_look.scss.
  Done: QuickLook component with dark backdrop overlay (blur + rgba), centered

  user starts typing. Revert to compact when query is cleared. Toggle via
  prop on CommandPalette. Animate height transition.

  animation (`grid-template-rows: 1fr → 0fr` transition). Opacity fade with

  height mostly opens; collapse: opacity fades immediately, visibility hidden

  Collapsible wrapper wraps body + ActionPanel; overlays (QuickLook, Alert,
  Toast, HUD) remain outside. Accessibility: `aria-hidden` and `inert` on
  collapsible wrapper when compact to prevent focus/screen-reader access to

- [ ] [P3] THEME003 - Add proper semantic color system. Create Color enum/object

  options. Handler receives KeyboardEvent for flexibility. Refactored App.tsx:
  replaced ad-hoc useEffect+addEventListener handlers for ⌘K (toggle actions),
  ⌘N (create snippet), and ⌘Enter (form submit) with useKeyboardShortcut calls.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] KBD002 - Add favorite items support. ⌘⇧F toggles favorite on
  selected item. Favorited items appear in a "Favorites" section at top of
  root search. Persist favorites in state (localStorage for web demo). Show
  star icon on favorited items. ⌘⌥↑/↓ reorders favorites.
  Done: useFavorites hook (src/hooks/use_favorites.ts) with localStorage

  tracking, "Add/Remove from Favorites" action in dropdown. Star accessory

- [x] [P2] KBD003 - Add ⌃N/⌃P alternative navigation. In addition to arrow

  walkdir 2, dirs-next 2, open 5, trash 5 (cross-platform safe trash). File
  type detection for code/image/pdf/archive/spreadsheet/presentation/document/
  folder. Path validation (is_path_within_home) on open/reveal/trash to prevent
  unauthorized file access. Hash-based file IDs from path for stable identity.
  PlatformFiles interface in types.ts, implemented in tauri.ts (invoke) and
  web.ts (noop fallback). FileSearchView: 250ms debounce on search IPC calls,
  race-safe request tracking, proper ErrorHUDIcon for failure HUDs,
  OpenInBrowserHUDIcon for success. reveal_in_finder checks exit status on all
  platforms. Removed .DS_Store from directory ignore list (it's a file). All 93
  tests pass, cargo check passes, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] TAURI006a - Rust-side: Add walkdir + tauri-plugin-shell + trash
    dependencies. Create search_files (async) Tauri command with recursive dir
    traversal, .git/node_modules/hidden exclusion, extension-based file type
    detection, file metadata. Add open_file, reveal_in_finder, move_to_trash
    commands with path validation (is_path_within_home). trash crate for
    cross-platform safe trash (replaces osascript injection vulnerability).
  - [x] TAURI006b - Update capabilities/default.json with shell:allow-open
    permission. Install @tauri-apps/plugin-shell JS package via bun.
  - [x] TAURI006c - Extend PlatformAPI: add PlatformFiles interface (searchFiles,
    openFile, revealInFinder, moveToTrash) to types.ts. Implement in tauri.ts
    (invoke Tauri commands) and web.ts (mock/noop fallbacks). Export from index.ts.
  - [x] TAURI006d - Refactor FileSearchView to use PlatformAPI for real file search
    in Tauri mode, mock data in web mode. Wire open/reveal/trash actions to real
    platform operations. Add 250ms debounce, loading state, ErrorHUDIcon for
    failure states.

- [x] [P1] TAURI007 - Add native app launching. Use @tauri-apps/api/shell
  to launch applications. On macOS: scan /Applications and ~/Applications
  for .app bundles, extract app name and icon. On Windows: scan Start Menu
  shortcuts. Create Rust command for app discovery that returns name, path,
  icon (base64 PNG). Cache app list and refresh on focus. Wire into root
  search: show matching apps in "Applications" section. Enter launches app
  and hides window.
  Done: Rust-side discover_applications (async, cached via AppCache mutex) and
  launch_application commands in lib.rs. App discovery: scan_app_dir with 1-level
  recursion, read_info_plist for single parse per app (consolidated from duplicate
  calls), display_name_from_plist for CFBundleDisplayName/CFBundleName, extract_app_icon
  from .icns files (PNG extraction with preferred small types: icp5/icp6/icp4/ic07,
  200KB max fallback to avoid huge 1024x1024 icons). base64 data URI encoding.
  is_valid_app_path security validation: canonicalize + restrict to /Applications and
  ~/Applications only. Hash-based IDs. Sorted alphabetically. Dependencies: plist 1,
  base64 0.22. PlatformApps interface (types.ts), Tauri invoke implementation (tauri.ts),
  web noop fallback (web.ts). useApplications hook with loading state, console.error
  on failure (no silent swallowing), cache-based refresh on window focus (not force).
  Root search: AppIcon component with onError fallback to AppPlaceholderIcon, SCSS class
  for border-radius (no inline styles), fuzzy search on app names, launch+hide on Enter,
  error toast on launch failure. All 93 tests pass, cargo check passes, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
    - [x] TAURI007a: Rust backend - discover_applications + launch_application commands +
      security (is_valid_app_path with canonicalize + allowed dirs restriction),
      consolidated plist parsing, icon size limits, AppCache with mutex.
    - [x] TAURI007b: Frontend platform API (types.ts, tauri.ts, web.ts) + AppEntry type,
      PlatformApps interface with discoverApplications/launchApplication.
    - [x] TAURI007c: Root search integration - useApplications hook with loading state,
      AppIcon with onError fallback, fuzzy search, cache refresh on focus, launch+hide.

- [x] [P2] TAURI008 - Add SQLite persistence layer via Tauri.
  Broken into sub-tasks:
  - [x] TAURI008a - Add tauri-plugin-sql dependency (Rust + JS), register
    plugin in lib.rs, configure permissions in capabilities. Verify plugin
    loads with empty DB.
  - [x] TAURI008b - Create database schema SQL and Rust-side migrations.
    Tables: clipboard_entries, snippets, quicklinks, notes, recent_commands,
    settings. Schema versioning via migrations table. Full-text search index
    on text content columns. FTS5 sync triggers for clipboard, snippets,
    quicklinks. JSON validation on tags columns. Auto-update trigger for
    notes.updated_at. All timestamps use ISO 8601 UTC format.
  - [x] TAURI008c - Create src/utils/database.ts with typed query helpers.
    Platform-aware: uses SQLite via Tauri when available, falls back to
    localStorage (existing storage.ts) on web. Typed insert/select/update/

  persisted in settings DB to avoid sync issues. All 93 tests pass,

- [ ] [P2] TAURI010 - Add auto-update system. Use @tauri-apps/plugin-updater
  for in-app updates. Configure update endpoint URL in tauri.conf.json.
  Check for updates on launch (silent) and via Settings > Advanced. Show
  toast notification when update available. "Install Update & Restart"
  action. Progress bar during download. Changelog display before install.
  Sign updates with Tauri's built-in code signing.

=== MISSING RAYCAST SCREENS ===

- [x] [P1] SCRN010 - Build Quicklinks screen. List view of saved quicklinks
  (URLs, file paths, deep links). Each item: icon (auto-detected from URL
  favicon or file type), name, URL/path as subtitle. Actions: Open (⏎),
  Edit (⌘E pushes form), Copy Link (⌘C), Delete (⌘⌫). Create quicklink
  form: name, link (URL/path/deep link), icon selection, application to
  open with. Support query placeholders in URLs (e.g. "https://google.com/
  search?q={query}" — when opened, prompts for query input). Push from
  "Search Quicklinks" command. Create src/data/quicklinks_data.ts with 10
  mock quicklinks and src/components/quicklinks/quicklinks_view.tsx.

- [x] [P1] SCRN011 - Build Window Management screen. Grid or list of window
  layout presets: Left Half, Right Half, Top Half, Bottom Half, Center,
  Maximize, First Third, Middle Third, Last Third, First Two Thirds, Last
  Two Thirds, Top Left Quarter, Top Right Quarter, Bottom Left Quarter,
  Bottom Right Quarter. Each preset shows a visual diagram (CSS box showing
  the layout position highlighted). Keyboard shortcuts for each position
  (configurable). In Tauri mode, use @tauri-apps/api/window to actually
  resize/reposition windows. In web mode, show visual demo only. Create
  src/components/window_management/window_management_view.tsx and
  src/data/window_layouts_data.ts.
  Done: WindowManagementView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, List,
  ActionPanel, HUDContainer). 16 window layout presets across 4 categories
  (Halves, Thirds, Quarters, Other) in window_layouts_data.tsx. Each layout
  has inline SVG visual diagram showing highlighted region on a monitor outline.
  List grouped by category with SearchDropdown filter. Colored category tags
  (blue/green/orange/purple). Keyboard shortcut badges (⌃⌥ combos) shown as
  Kbd accessories. Apply Layout on Enter with HUD, Copy Name via ⌘C. ⌘K
  actions dropdown. Fuzzy search filtering. selectedIndex bounds clamping.
  Single-pass Map grouping algorithm. WindowManagementCommandIcon with
  gradient background. Registered in command registry with fullView:true.
  Web mode: visual demo (HUD feedback). Tauri integration deferred to
  TAURI platform tasks. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] SCRN012 - Build Floating Notes screen. Markdown-enabled note
  editor that floats above other apps. In-memory list of notes with titles.
  Notes list view with search. Click note to open editor. Editor: simple
  textarea with markdown preview toggle. Auto-save on change (debounced
  500ms). Create new note via ⌘N. Delete note with confirm. Persist notes
  via localStorage/SQLite. Push from "Floating Notes" command. Create
  src/components/floating_notes/floating_notes_view.tsx and
  floating_notes_editor.tsx.
  Done: FloatingNotesView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, List, ActionPanel,
  Alert, HUDContainer). 10 mock note entries in notes_data.ts. NoteEditorView
  subcomponent with debounced auto-save (500ms), markdown preview toggle
  (⌘⇧P) using marked + DOMPurify with detail__markdown styling, edit/preview
  mode switch button. Notes list: Recent/Older sections (24h threshold),
  fuzzy search + FTS5 via useDbSearch, DocumentIcon per item, relative date
  accessories. Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘K actions, ⌘C copy, ⌘N create,
  ⌘⌫ delete with confirm, ⌘S flush save, Escape back to list. NotesIcon
  (golden gradient) added to icon system. Extension registered in
  extensions/index.ts. Database persistence via noteDb (CRUD). Consistent
  renderNow for date formatting. TrashIcon from icon system for delete alert.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] SCRN013 - Build Translator screen. Two-panel layout: source text
  (left/top) and translated text (right/bottom). Language selector dropdowns
  for source and target. Swap languages button. Auto-detect source language.
  Copy translation button. In web demo mode, use a simple mock translation
  (reverse text or placeholder). In production, integrate with translation
  API. Support 20+ languages. Create src/components/translator/
  translator_view.tsx and translator.scss. Push from "Translate" command.
  Done: TranslatorView with self-contained UI (SearchBar, ActionPanel,
  HUDContainer). Two-panel flex layout with source textarea and translation
  output. SearchDropdown language selectors for both source (26 langs incl
  auto-detect) and target (25 langs) in panel headers. Swap languages button
  (disabled when source is auto). Auto-detect via regex patterns for CJK,
  Cyrillic, Arabic, Hindi, Thai + keyword scoring with global regex for
  Romance languages. Mock translation engine: known phrases → word-swap
  dictionary → prefixed passthrough. 300ms debounce on translation. History
  sub-view with searchable list, fuzzy filtering, load-from-history. Keyboard
  shortcuts: ⌘K actions, ⌘C copy translation (guards against textarea focus
  to preserve native copy), ⌘S save to history, ⌘⇧H show history, Escape
  back. TranslateIcon (blue gradient) added to icon system. Extension
  registered with fullView:true in Suggestions section. All SCSS values
  tokenized (local vars for panel-header-height, textarea-min-height,
  swap-button-size, line-height-body, disabled-opacity). All 93 tests pass,
  bun run check passes.

- [x] [P2] SCRN014 - Build Reminders screen. List of reminders with
  title, due date/time, and repeat options. Create reminder form: title,
  date (FormDatePicker), time, repeat (none/daily/weekly/monthly), notes.
  Sections: Overdue, Today, Upcoming, Completed. Toggle complete with
  checkbox. Delete with confirm. In Tauri mode, use OS notification API

- [ ] [P2] COMP019 - Build Action.PickDate component. Date picker that
  appears inline in action panel or as a pushed form field. Supports
  date-only and date+time modes. Min/max date constraints. Calendar grid
  UI with month/year navigation. Selected date highlighted with accent.
  Keyboard navigation: arrows for days, PageUp/Down for months. Create
  src/components/date_picker/ with date_picker.tsx and date_picker.scss.

- [ ] [P2] COMP020 - Add List pagination support (infinite scroll). Add
  onLoadMore callback and hasMore boolean props to List. When user scrolls
  near bottom (IntersectionObserver on sentinel element), trigger
  onLoadMore. Show loading skeleton items (3 placeholder rows with pulse
  animation) while loading. Update List component. Demo: Clipboard History
  loads 20 items at a time.

- [x] [P2] COMP021 - Build proper markdown renderer for Detail component.
  Already implemented: Detail component (detail.tsx) uses marked + dompurify
  with DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(...)). Comprehensive SCSS styles in
  detail.scss covering: headings (h1-h6), bold/italic (via markdown), code
  (inline + blocks with $font-family-mono), links (accent color + hover),
  lists (ordered + unordered), blockquotes (left border + muted color),
  tables (full width, collapsed borders, header styling), horizontal rules,
  images (max-width, rounded). No separate wrapper needed — single usage
  in Detail component makes a utility file unnecessary.

- [ ] [P2] COMP022 - Build Form.LinkAccessory component. Small accessory
  displayed in the right side of the SearchBar/navigation bar when a Form
  is active. Shows a link with icon + label. Clickable, opens URL or
  triggers action. Create src/components/form/form_link_accessory.tsx.
  Update SearchBar to accept optional rightAccessory prop. Demo: "View Docs"
  link in Create Snippet form.

- [ ] [P3] COMP023 - Build color swatch accessory for ListItem. When a
  ListItem accessory has a color hex value, render a small color circle
  (12x12px, rounded) next to the text. Used in clipboard history for copied
  colors and in color picker. Update ListItem rendering to detect color
  accessory type and render swatch. Add color?: string field to
  ListItemAccessoryData.

- [ ] [P3] COMP024 - Build empty search fallback actions. When root search
  has no matching results, show fallback items: "Search Google for '{query}'",
  "Search DuckDuckGo for '{query}'", "Search Wikipedia for '{query}'". Each
  item has appropriate search engine icon. Enter opens search URL in browser.
  Configurable fallback list in settings. Create src/data/fallback_data.ts.
  Update App.tsx to show fallbacks when filteredSections is empty.

=== VISUAL POLISH ===

- [ ] [P2] VIS001 - Add virtual scrolling for large lists. Current List
  renders all items in DOM which becomes slow with 100+ items. Implement
  virtual scroll: only render visible items + buffer (5 items above/below
  viewport). Calculate item positions from fixed height ($list-item-height).
  Maintain scroll position on item count changes. Keep keyboard navigation
  working (scrollIntoView on active item). Create src/hooks/use_virtual_
  scroll.ts. Apply to List component. Test with 1000+ items in Clipboard
  History.

- [ ] [P2] VIS002 - Add custom window title bar. Since Tauri uses frameless
  window, build a custom title bar. macOS style: traffic light buttons
  (close/minimize/maximize) on left side, centered title text. Make it
  draggable (data-tauri-drag-region). In web mode, hide title bar (browser
  has its own). Create src/components/title_bar/ with title_bar.tsx and
  title_bar.scss. Position above CommandPalette. 28px height matching macOS.

- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists
  should have native-feeling scroll momentum (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth).
  When scroll stops, snap to nearest item boundary (CSS scroll-snap-type:
  y proximity on list container, scroll-snap-align: start on items). Ensure
  keyboard navigation scrollIntoView uses smooth behavior. Test on macOS
  trackpad and mouse wheel.

- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring
  that transitions between items (not just instant highlight switch).
  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.
  The ring should have a very subtle glow (box-shadow with accent color at
  10% opacity). 80ms transition duration. Only show on keyboard navigation,
  not mouse hover.

- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette
  appears: macOS-style spring animation (scale from 0.95 to 1.0, opacity
  0 to 1, with slight overshoot). When closing: scale to 0.97, opacity to 0,
  faster (150ms). Use CSS @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing that mimics
  spring physics. In Tauri mode, coordinate with window show/hide timing.

- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a
  GridItem for 800ms, show an enlarged preview tooltip above the item
  (larger icon/image, full title, metadata). Positioned above the item,
  centered. Fade in/out. Dismiss on mouse leave. Create
  src/components/grid/grid_preview.tsx and grid_preview.scss.

- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.
  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.

=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match

  - [x] INFRA004a - Create ExtensionManifest and ExtensionCommand types in
    types.ts. Add registerExtension() to command_registry.ts. Export from index.ts.
  - [x] INFRA004b - Create src/extensions/ directory with manifest files for
    each built-in extension: clipboard, snippets, file-search, quicklinks,
    emoji, window-management, color-picker, settings, and applications bundle.
  - [x] INFRA004c - Replace commands.ts with barrel import from extensions/,
    create extensions/index.ts for auto-discovery. Clean up dead code.

- [x] [P1] INFRA005 - Create Tauri IPC bridge layer. Abstract all
  platform-specific operations behind a unified interface. Create
  src/platform/index.ts with PlatformAPI interface: clipboard (read/write/
  monitor), files (search/open/trash), apps (list/launch), shell (execute),
  window (show/hide/position), notifications (send). Two implementations:
  src/platform/web.ts (browser fallbacks using navigator, localStorage, etc)
  and src/platform/tauri.ts (real native via @tauri-apps/api). Auto-detect
  environment and export correct implementation. All views import from
  platform/index.ts only.
  Done: PlatformAPI interface with PlatformClipboard, PlatformShell, and
  PlatformWindow interfaces in src/platform/types.ts. Web implementation
  (src/platform/web.ts) uses navigator.clipboard, window.open, anchor clicks.
  Tauri implementation (src/platform/tauri.ts) uses dynamic imports for
  @tauri-apps/api/window, @tauri-apps/api/core, @tauri-apps/api/event.
  Auto-detection via __TAURI_INTERNALS__ check. Race-safe initPlatform() with
  cached promise. getPlatform() synchronous accessor (throws if Tauri not
  initialized). main.tsx awaits initPlatform() before createRoot. clipboard.ts,
  open_url.ts, use_window.ts refactored to delegate to getPlatform(). Shell
  methods are async (Promise<void>) for future native Tauri shell integration.
  Unsubscribe type for cleanup callbacks. isTauri exported from platform/index.ts
  and re-exported from use_window.ts for backward compatibility. Tauri clipboard/
  shell use web APIs as fallback (proper native implementations deferred to
  TAURI005-007). All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] INFRA005a - Create platform API types and implementations
    (src/platform/types.ts, src/platform/web.ts, src/platform/tauri.ts,
    src/platform/index.ts). PlatformAPI covers: clipboard (writeText/readText),
    shell (openUrl/openDeepLink as async), window (hide/setPositionPref/
    onFocusChanged/onTrayNavigate/syncStoredPositionToBackend).
  - [x] INFRA005b - Refactor existing utils to use platform API: clipboard.ts,
    open_url.ts, use_window.ts, main.tsx. Consumers unchanged.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA006 - Add error boundary components. Create
  src/components/error_boundary/ with error_boundary.tsx (class component
  wrapping React.ErrorBoundary). Show friendly error UI: icon, "Something
  went wrong" title, error message (collapsed by default, expandable),
  "Try Again" button that resets state. Style matching Raycast error states.
  Wrap each pushed view in error boundary. Log errors to console with
  component stack. Create error_boundary.scss.

- [ ] [P2] INFRA007 - Add state management with @belt/tools state-machine.
Replace prop-drilling and scattered useState across App - belt/tools is an
internal package public available in npm. See docs and examples at /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools

- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite
  production build: minification (terser), tree-shaking, chunk splitting
  (vendor chunk for React, component chunks per route/view). Configure
  asset hashing for cache busting. Add bundle size analysis (rollup-plugin-
  visualizer). Set up path aliases (@/ for src/). Add environment variable
  support (.env, .env.production). Target: initial bundle < 200KB gzipped.
  Update vite.config.ts.

- [x] [P1] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional
  Playwright visual tests. Run on push to main and PRs. Cache bun
  dependencies. Fail on lint errors or test failures. Add status badges
  to README. For Tauri: add tauri-build job that creates platform-specific
  binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage).
  Done: Created .github/workflows/ci.yml with 4 jobs: lint (type check + eslint
  + prettier --check), test (vitest), build (vite build), tauri-build (macOS
  aarch64 + x86_64). Bun dependency caching via actions/cache with bun.lock hash
  key. Rust caching via Swatinem/rust-cache. Concurrency: cancel-in-progress for
  PRs only (main runs complete). Timeout: 10min for frontend jobs, 30min for Tauri
  builds. CI lint uses `bunx eslint src` (no --fix) to catch all issues. Format
  check uses `bunx prettier --check .` (read-only). Tauri builds macOS-only
  (project uses window-vibrancy, plist, macOS private API). tauri-action pinned to
  @v0.5 (not floating @v0). Artifact upload for .dmg and .app bundles. Added
  .prettierignore (dist, src-tauri/target, src-tauri/gen) to speed up prettier.
  Linux/Windows builds deferred until cross-platform support is implemented.
  All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA010 - Add telemetry/analytics abstraction. Create
  src/utils/analytics.ts with track(event, properties?) function. Events:
  command_opened, search_query, action_triggered, extension_enabled/disabled,
  theme_changed. In web demo: log to console. In production: send to
  analytics endpoint. Respect user opt-out preference in settings. Add
  "Share Usage Data" toggle in Settings > Advanced. No PII in events.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create
  src/i18n/ with translation files (en.json, pt.json, es.json, ja.json).
  Translation keys for all UI strings (button labels, section titles,
  placeholder texts, error messages). Create useTranslation hook that
  returns t(key) function. Language selector in Settings > General. Default
  to system locale. Persist preference. Start with English strings
  extracted from all components.

content.text:
## Understanding of progress.txt

The project is a Raycast UI clone built with React 19 + Vite 6 + Sass + TypeScript + Tauri v2. Extensive progress has been made:

**Completed:** All P0 tasks, most P1 and P2 tasks including core components (Alert, Navigation, ActionPanel sections/submenus, Dropdowns, HUD, Form components, Tooltips, QuickLook, 50+ icons), full screens (Clipboard History, Snippet Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search, Settings, Quicklinks, Window Management, Floating Notes, Translator, Reminders), themes (light/dark), compact mode, keyboard system, fuzzy search, extension architecture, Tauri integration (global hotkey, tray, clipboard, file system, app launching, SQLite, auto-launch, CI/CD).

**Pending tasks by priority:**

- **[P2]**: TAURI010 (auto-update), COMP019 (date picker), COMP020 (infinite scroll), COMP022 (form link accessory), INFRA006 (error boundary), INFRA007 (state management), INFRA008 (build config), VIS001-VIS004 (virtual scroll, title bar, scroll snap, focus ring), SRCH004 (⌘L copy), SRCH005 (search debounce)
- **[P3]**: SCRN007 (Extension Store), SCRN008 (AI Chat), SCRN009 (Notes editor), SCRN015 (Script Commands), THEME003 (semantic colors), COMP023 (color swatch), COMP024 (empty search fallback), VIS005-VIS007, SRCH006 (Tab completion), INFRA010 (analytics), INFRA011 (i18n)
- **[P1000]**: TEST001-TEST006

The highest priority pending tasks are all **[P2]**. Let me pick the most impactful one. INFRA006 (Error Boundary) is a good candidate — it's a safety net that prevents the whole app from crashing, it's well-scoped (~2-3 files), and it's infrastructure that benefits everything else.

Let me start with **INFRA006 - Add error boundary components**.

  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch

input.prompt:
I need to build an error boundary component for a Raycast clone app. Research the following and return findings:

1. List all files in src/components/ directories to understand the component structure pattern
2. Read src/styles/_tokens.scss to understand available design tokens
3. Read src/components/alert/alert.tsx and alert.scss to understand the similar overlay/modal pattern (error boundary will have a similar visual treatment)
4. Read src/App.tsx or src/components/root_search/root_search_view.tsx to understand where pushed views are rendered (error boundaries need to wrap those)
5. Read src/hooks/use_navigation.ts to understand the navigation push/pop mechanism
6. Check if there's a WarningIcon or similar in the icon system

Return all the relevant design tokens, patterns, and code structure details I need.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Research Findings: Error Boundary Component Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of your Raycast clone codebase, here's a complete analysis of patterns, design tokens, and structures you'll need for building an error boundary component.

---

### 1. COMPONENT STRUCTURE PATTERN

**Directories in `/src/components/`:**
- Alert, Toast, HUD (notification/modal patterns you should follow)
- Each component has: `component.tsx` + `component.scss`
- File naming: `error_boundary.tsx` + `error_boundary.scss`

**Key Takeaway**: Your project uses a flat component structure with colocated styles. Each component folder contains:
- Main component file (e.g., `alert.tsx`)
- Styles file (e.g., `alert.scss`)
- Optional supporting types/utilities

---

### 2. DESIGN TOKENS (from `_tokens.scss`)

**Spacing (all use `$space-*` pattern):**
- `$space-half: 2px`
- `$space-1: 4px`
- `$space-2: 8px`
- `$space-3: 12px`
- `$space-4: 16px`
- `$space-5: 20px` (commonly used for padding)
- `$space-6: 24px`
- `$space-8: 32px` (used for icon containers)

**Border Radius:**
- `$radius-md: 8px` (buttons, small modals)
- `$radius-lg: 12px` (dialog containers)
- `$radius-window: 18px` (for CommandPalette wrapper)

**Typography:**
- `$font-sm: 12px` (message text)
- `$font-md: 13px` (titles)
- `$font-base: 14px` (body)
- `$font-family`: Inter system font stack

**Font Weights:**
- `$font-weight-medium: 500` (buttons, secondary)
- `$font-weight-semibold: 600` (titles)

**Transitions:**
- `$transition-fast: 80ms` (quick interactions)
- `$transition-normal: 150ms` (standard animations)
- `$transition-slow: 250ms` (entrance/exit)

**Z-Index:**
- `$z-alert: 200` (highest modal overlay)
- `$z-quick-look: 130`
- `$z-hud: 120`

---

### 3. ALERT COMPONENT PATTERN (Your Blueprint)

**Alert is the closest pattern to error boundary. Key learnings:**

**React Structure** (`alert.tsx`):
```typescript
- Interface: AlertOptions { title, message?, icon?, primaryAction, dismissAction? }
- Exit animation constant: EXIT_ANIMATION_MS = 150
- State management: exiting boolean (for animation)
- useRef for dialog, primary button, callback refs
- useId for aria-labelledby/aria-describedby
- Keyboard handling: Escape dismisses, Tab wraps focus, Shift+Tab reverses
- Backdrop click handling with delegation check (e.target === e.currentTarget)
```

**SCSS Pattern** (`alert.scss`):
- `.alert__backdrop`: absolute positioned, inset: 0, flexbox center, uses CSS variables
- `.alert__dialog`: flex column, centered, width-constrained, backdrop-filter blur
- Icon/Title/Message/Actions layered vertically
- Animations: backdrop-enter/exit, dialog-enter/exit (scale + opacity)
- Button states: `--default`, `--destructive`, `--cancel` with hover effects
- Focus visible: 2px solid outline with negative offset

---

### 4. AVAILABLE CSS VARIABLES (from `global.scss`)

**Colors:**
- `--color-background: #191919` (dark) / `#ececec` (light)
- `--color-surface: #232325` (dark) / `#ffffff` (light)
- `--color-surface-hover: #2a2a2d` / `#f0f0f2`
- `--color-text-primary: #e5e5ea` / `#1d1d1f`
- `--color-accent: #007aff` (consistent across themes)
- `--color-destructive: #ff453a` (dark) / `#ff3b30` (light)
- `--color-destructive-hover: #ff5e55` / `#e5342b`
- `--color-success: #30d158` / `#28cd41`
- `--text-600: rgba(229,229,234,0.6)` / rgba theme variant
- `--border-100/200`: Subtle borders with opacity

**Shadows & Effects:**
- `--shadow-window`: 0 24px 80px + 0 2px 12px (heavy shadow)
- `--shadow-popover`: 0 12px 32px + 0 1px 6px (light shadow)
- `--backdrop-overlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` (dark) / `rgba(0,0,0,0.2)` (light)
- `--backdrop-blur: 4px`

---

### 5. NAVIGATION & VIEW RENDERING ARCHITECTURE

**Navigation Stack Pattern** (`use_navigation.ts`):
```typescript
- NavigationContext<T> with: push(), pop(), popToRoot()
- currentEntry: NavigationEntry<T> | null
- breadcrumbs: BreadcrumbItem[] (with optional onBack callbacks)
- canGoBack, direction ('push'|'pop'|'none'), stackDepth, navKey
- Used as: useNavigation<NavViewData>()
```

**View Rendering** (`App.tsx`):
- CommandPalette wraps all views
- Views rendered in `renderNavView()` function
- Two patterns: full-view (single component) or split-view (search + list + detail)
- Navigation data types: `type: 'command'` or `type: 'detail'`

**Error Boundary Placement:**
Error boundaries should wrap:
1. The **CommandPalette component** (wraps all nav views)
2. Individual **pushed views** (around `renderNavView` output)

---

### 6. ICON SYSTEM

**Icon Architecture:**
- Icons in `/src/components/icons/` organized by category
- Categories: `action_icons.tsx`, `arrow_icons.tsx`, `control_icons.tsx`, `icons.tsx`
- **No built-in warning/error icon** - you'll need to create one
- Icons use `svgBase(props)` utility for consistent viewBox and sizing
- Default size: 16px (controlled icons) or 20px (larger icons)

**Icon Pattern:**
```typescript
export function AlertIcon(props: IconProps = {}) {
  return (
    <svg {...svgBase(props)}>
      {/* SVG content, uses currentColor for color */}
    </svg>
  );
}
```

**Recommended Error Icon Styling:**
- Use `STROKE_DEFAULTS` from `icon_shared.ts` for consistent styling
- Or create a filled icon with an exclamation mark/warning symbol
- Should be colorable via `currentColor`

---

### 7. TOAST vs ALERT vs ERROR BOUNDARY (Pattern Comparison)

| Aspect | Toast | Alert | Error Boundary |
|--------|-------|-------|---|
| Duration | Auto-dismiss | User action | Persistent |
| Interaction | Click to dismiss | Buttons + keyboard | Buttons required |
| Overlay | Slide in (corner) | Modal backdrop | Modal backdrop |
| Z-index | Lower (120+) | Highest (200) | Should match Alert (200) |
| Use Case | Notifications | Confirmations | Critical failures |
| Animation | Slide + fade | Scale + fade | Scale + fade |

---

### 8. DETAILED FILE REFERENCES

**Key component implementations to study:**

1. **Error UI**: `/src/components/alert/alert.tsx` (154 lines)
   - Shows animation pattern, keyboard handling, focus management
   
2. **Error Styling**: `/src/components/alert/alert.scss` (154 lines)
   - Backdrop, dialog, button styles, keyframe animations
   
3. **Navigation Context**: `/src/hooks/use_navigation.ts` (182 lines)
   - Shows how views are stacked and rendered
   
4. **View Rendering**: `/src/App.tsx` (147 lines)
   - Shows where error boundary should wrap
   - Shows CommandPalette as primary wrapper

5. **Toast Pattern**: `/src/components/toast/toast.tsx` (105 lines)
   - Shows status vs alert role distinction
   - Shows aria-live attributes

---

### 9. SPECIFIC DESIGN RECOMMENDATIONS FOR ERROR BOUNDARY

**Visual Treatment:**
- Use same scale/fade animation as Alert (`alert-dialog-enter/exit`)
- Z-index: `$z-alert: 200` (highest modal layer)
- Border radius: `$radius-lg: 12px` for container
- Backdrop: `var(--backdrop-overlay)` with `backdrop-filter: blur(var(--backdrop-blur))`
- Shadow: `var(--shadow-popover)` (lighter than window shadow)

**Typography:**
- Title: `$font-md` (13px), `$font-weight-semibold` (600)
- Message: `$font-sm` (12px), `color: var(--text-600)`
- Button: `$font-sm` (12px), `$font-weight-medium` (500)

**Spacing:**
- Dialog padding: `$space-5 $space-4 $space-4` (20px top, 16px sides)
- Icon size: `$space-8` (32px) with `margin-bottom: $space-3` (12px)
- Actions gap: `$space-2` (8px)
- Button height: `$space-8` (32px)

**Interactivity:**
- Primary action button: `var(--color-destructive)` for errors
- Secondary action: `var(--border-100)` background
- Hover: `var(--color-destructive-hover)` or `var(--border-200)`
- Focus visible: `2px solid var(--color-accent)` with `outline-offset: -2px`

**Accessibility:**
- `role="alertdialog"` on dialog
- `aria-modal="true"`
- `aria-labelledby` for title
- `aria-describedby` for message
- Keyboard: Escape to dismiss, Tab to navigate buttons
- Focus trap: Last button Tab wraps to first button

---

### 10. COMPONENT LOCATION & INTEGRATION POINTS

**Recommended structure:**
```
src/components/
├── error_boundary/
│   ├── error_boundary.tsx
│   ├── error_boundary.scss
│   └── use_error_boundary.ts (custom hook)
```

**Integration points:**
1. **App-level wrapper**: Wrap `<CommandPalette>` in App.tsx
2. **View-level wrapper**: Optional, wrap individual pushed views
3. **Hook export**: Export `useErrorBoundary()` for throwing errors from components

**Usage pattern** (based on Alert pattern):
```typescript
- ErrorBoundary component takes children
- Shows error dialog with title, message, optional icon
- onDismiss callback for cleanup
- Reset/Retry button for recovery
```

---

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report.

## Research Findings: Error Boundary Component Architecture

Based on my thorough exploration of your Raycast clone codebase, here's a complete analysis of patterns, design tokens, and structures you'll need for building an error boundary component.

---

### 1. COMPONENT STRUCTURE PATTERN

**Directories in `/src/components/`:**
- Alert, Toast, HUD (notification/modal patterns you should follow)
- Each component has: `component.tsx` + `component.scss`
- File naming: `error_boundary.tsx` + `error_boundary.scss`

**Key Takeaway**: Your project uses a flat component structure with colocated styles. Each component folder contains:
- Main component file (e.g., `alert.tsx`)
- Styles file (e.g., `alert.scss`)
- Optional supporting types/utilities

---

### 2. DESIGN TOKENS (from `_tokens.scss`)

**Spacing (all use `$space-*` pattern):**
- `$space-half: 2px`
- `$space-1: 4px`
- `$space-2: 8px`
- `$space-3: 12px`
- `$space-4: 16px`
- `$space-5: 20px` (commonly used for padding)
- `$space-6: 24px`
- `$space-8: 32px` (used for icon containers)

**Border Radius:**
- `$radius-md: 8px` (buttons, small modals)
- `$radius-lg: 12px` (dialog containers)
- `$radius-window: 18px` (for CommandPalette wrapper)

**Typography:**
- `$font-sm: 12px` (message text)
- `$font-md: 13px` (titles)
- `$font-base: 14px` (body)
- `$font-family`: Inter system font stack

**Font Weights:**
- `$font-weight-medium: 500` (buttons, secondary)
- `$font-weight-semibold: 600` (titles)

**Transitions:**
- `$transition-fast: 80ms` (quick interactions)
- `$transition-normal: 150ms` (standard animations)
- `$transition-slow: 250ms` (entrance/exit)

**Z-Index:**
- `$z-alert: 200` (highest modal overlay)
- `$z-quick-look: 130`
- `$z-hud: 120`

---

### 3. ALERT COMPONENT PATTERN (Your Blueprint)

**Alert is the closest pattern to error boundary. Key learnings:**

**React Structure** (`alert.tsx`):
```typescript
- Interface: AlertOptions { title, message?, icon?, primaryAction, dismissAction? }
- Exit animation constant: EXIT_ANIMATION_MS = 150
- State management: exiting boolean (for animation)
- useRef for dialog, primary button, callback refs
- useId for aria-labelledby/aria-describedby
- Keyboard handling: Escape dismisses, Tab wraps focus, Shift+Tab reverses
- Backdrop click handling with delegation check (e.target === e.currentTarget)
```

**SCSS Pattern** (`alert.scss`):
- `.alert__backdrop`: absolute positioned, inset: 0, flexbox center, uses CSS variables
- `.alert__dialog`: flex column, centered, width-constrained, backdrop-filter blur
- Icon/Title/Message/Actions layered vertically
- Animations: backdrop-enter/exit, dialog-enter/exit (scale + opacity)
- Button states: `--default`, `--destructive`, `--cancel` with hover effects
- Focus visible: 2px solid outline with negative offset

---

### 4. AVAILABLE CSS VARIABLES (from `global.scss`)

**Colors:**
- `--color-background: #191919` (dark) / `#ececec` (light)
- `--color-surface: #232325` (dark) / `#ffffff` (light)
- `--color-surface-hover: #2a2a2d` / `#f0f0f2`
- `--color-text-primary: #e5e5ea` / `#1d1d1f`
- `--color-accent: #007aff` (consistent across themes)
- `--color-destructive: #ff453a` (dark) / `#ff3b30` (light)
- `--color-destructive-hover: #ff5e55` / `#e5342b`
- `--color-success: #30d158` / `#28cd41`
- `--text-600: rgba(229,229,234,0.6)` / rgba theme variant
- `--border-100/200`: Subtle borders with opacity

**Shadows & Effects:**
- `--shadow-window`: 0 24px 80px + 0 2px 12px (heavy shadow)
- `--shadow-popover`: 0 12px 32px + 0 1px 6px (light shadow)
- `--backdrop-overlay: rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` (dark) / `rgba(0,0,0,0.2)` (light)
- `--backdrop-blur: 4px`

---

### 5. NAVIGATION & VIEW RENDERING ARCHITECTURE

**Navigation Stack Pattern** (`use_navigation.ts`):
```typescript
- NavigationContext<T> with: push(), pop(), popToRoot()
- currentEntry: NavigationEntry<T> | null
- breadcrumbs: BreadcrumbItem[] (with optional onBack callbacks)
- canGoBack, direction ('push'|'pop'|'none'), stackDepth, navKey
- Used as: useNavigation<NavViewData>()
```

**View Rendering** (`App.tsx`):
- CommandPalette wraps all views
- Views rendered in `renderNavView()` function
- Two patterns: full-view (single component) or split-view (search + list + detail)
- Navigation data types: `type: 'command'` or `type: 'detail'`

**Error Boundary Placement:**
Error boundaries should wrap:
1. The **CommandPalette component** (wraps all nav views)
2. Individual **pushed views** (around `renderNavView` output)

---

### 6. ICON SYSTEM

**Icon Architecture:**
- Icons in `/src/components/icons/` organized by category
- Categories: `action_icons.tsx`, `arrow_icons.tsx`, `control_icons.tsx`, `icons.tsx`
- **No built-in warning/error icon** - you'll need to create one
- Icons use `svgBase(props)` utility for consistent viewBox and sizing
- Default size: 16px (controlled icons) or 20px (larger icons)

**Icon Pattern:**
```typescript
export function AlertIcon(props: IconProps = {}) {
  return (
    <svg {...svgBase(props)}>
      {/* SVG content, uses currentColor for color */}
    </svg>
  );
}
```

**Recommended Error Icon Styling:**
- Use `STROKE_DEFAULTS` from `icon_shared.ts` for consistent styling
- Or create a filled icon with an exclamation mark/warning symbol
- Should be colorable via `currentColor`

---

### 7. TOAST vs ALERT vs ERROR BOUNDARY (Pattern Comparison)

| Aspect | Toast | Alert | Error Boundary |
|--------|-------|-------|---|
| Duration | Auto-dismiss | User action | Persistent |
| Interaction | Click to dismiss | Buttons + keyboard | Buttons required |
| Overlay | Slide in (corner) | Modal backdrop | Modal backdrop |
| Z-index | Lower (120+) | Highest (200) | Should match Alert (200) |
| Use Case | Notifications | Confirmations | Critical failures |
| Animation | Slide + fade | Scale + fade | Scale + fade |

---

### 8. DETAILED FILE REFERENCES

**Key component implementations to study:**

1. **Error UI**: `/src/components/alert/alert.tsx` (154 lines)
   - Shows animation pattern, keyboard handling, focus management
   
2. **Error Styling**: `/src/components/alert/alert.scss` (154 lines)
   - Backdrop, dialog, button styles, keyframe animations
   
3. **Navigation Context**: `/src/hooks/use_navigation.ts` (182 lines)
   - Shows how views are stacked and rendered
   
4. **View Rendering**: `/src/App.tsx` (147 lines)
   - Shows where error boundary should wrap
   - Shows CommandPalette as primary wrapper

5. **Toast Pattern**: `/src/components/toast/toast.tsx` (105 lines)
   - Shows status vs alert role distinction
   - Shows aria-live attributes

---

### 9. SPECIFIC DESIGN RECOMMENDATIONS FOR ERROR BOUNDARY

**Visual Treatment:**
- Use same scale/fade animation as Alert (`alert-dialog-enter/exit`)
- Z-index: `$z-alert: 200` (highest modal layer)
- Border radius: `$radius-lg: 12px` for container
- Backdrop: `var(--backdrop-overlay)` with `backdrop-filter: blur(var(--backdrop-blur))`
- Shadow: `var(--shadow-popover)` (lighter than window shadow)

**Typography:**
- Title: `$font-md` (13px), `$font-weight-semibold` (600)
- Message: `$font-sm` (12px), `color: var(--text-600)`
- Button: `$font-sm` (12px), `$font-weight-medium` (500)

**Spacing:**
- Dialog padding: `$space-5 $space-4 $space-4` (20px top, 16px sides)
- Icon size: `$space-8` (32px) with `margin-bottom: $space-3` (12px)
- Actions gap: `$space-2` (8px)
- Button height: `$space-8` (32px)

**Interactivity:**
- Primary action button: `var(--color-destructive)` for errors
- Secondary action: `var(--border-100)` background
- Hover: `var(--color-destructive-hover)` or `var(--border-200)`
- Focus visible: `2px solid var(--color-accent)` with `outline-offset: -2px`

**Accessibility:**
- `role="alertdialog"` on dialog
- `aria-modal="true"`
- `aria-labelledby` for title
- `aria-describedby` for message
- Keyboard: Escape to dismiss, Tab to navigate buttons
- Focus trap: Last button Tab wraps to first button

---

### 10. COMPONENT LOCATION & INTEGRATION POINTS

**Recommended structure:**
```
src/components/
├── error_boundary/
│   ├── error_boundary.tsx
│   ├── error_boundary.scss
│   └── use_error_boundary.ts (custom hook)
```

**Integration points:**
1. **App-level wrapper**: Wrap `<CommandPalette>` in App.tsx
2. **View-level wrapper**: Optional, wrap individual pushed views
3. **Hook export**: Export `useErrorBoundary()` for throwing errors from components

**Usage pattern** (based on Alert pattern):
```typescript
- ErrorBoundary component takes children
- Shows error dialog with title, message, optional icon
- onDismiss callback for cleanup
- Reset/Retry button for recovery
```

---

### 11. FINAL CHECKLIST FOR IMPLEMENTATION

Design tokens to use:
- [x] Spacing: `$space-3` through `$space-8` for layout
- [x] Colors: CSS variables (surface, text-primary, text-600, destructive)
- [x] Radius: `$radius-lg` for dialog
- [x] Typography: `$font-md`, `$font-sm`, `$font-weight-semibold`
- [x] Shadows: `var(--shadow-popover)`
- [x] Transitions: `$transition-normal` (150ms)
- [x] Z-index: `$z-alert` (200)

Patterns to implement:
- [x] Alert-style backdrop + dialog structure
- [x] Scale + opacity animations (enter/exit)
- [x] Keyboard handling (Escape, Tab focus trap)
- [x] Backdrop click dismissal
- [x] Focus management (autofocus primary button)
- [x] ARIA attributes (alertdialog role, labelledby/describedby)
- [x] Button style variants (default, destructive, cancel)

export type { IconProps } from './icon_shared';

export {
  CalculatorIcon,
  ChatIcon,
  ClipboardIcon,
  CpuIcon,
  EmojiIcon,
  FigmaIcon,
  NotesIcon,
  PaletteIcon,
  RemindersIcon,
  SafariIcon,
  SlackIcon,
  SnippetsIcon,
  TerminalIcon,
  TranslateIcon,
  VscodeIcon,
  WindowIcon,
} from './icons';

export {
  ArrowDownIcon,
  ArrowLeftIcon,
  ArrowRightIcon,
  ArrowUpIcon,
  ChevronDownIcon,
  ChevronLeftIcon,
  ChevronRightIcon,
  ChevronUpIcon,
} from './arrow_icons';

export {
  CheckIcon,
  CircleIcon,
  DotIcon,
  EllipsisIcon,
  MinusIcon,
  PlusIcon,
} from './control_icons';

export {
  CopyIcon,
  DownloadIcon,
  DuplicateIcon,
  EditIcon,
  PasteIcon,
  RefreshIcon,
  TrashIcon,
  UploadIcon,
} from './action_icons';

export {
  CalendarIcon,
  ClockIcon,
  DocumentIcon,
  FolderIcon,
  GearIcon,
  GlobeIcon,
  HeartIcon,
  KeyIcon,
  LinkIcon,
  LockIcon,
  MagnifyingGlassIcon,
  StarFilledIcon,
  StarIcon,
} from './object_icons';

export { BatteryIcon, BoltIcon, CloudIcon, WifiIcon } from './status_icons';

input.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Review the following changes for the INFRA006 task — adding an ErrorBoundary component to a Raycast clone app. The task specification:

"Add error boundary components. Create src/components/error_boundary/ with error_boundary.tsx (class component). Show friendly error UI: icon, 'Something went wrong' title, error message (collapsed by default, expandable), 'Try Again' button that resets state. Style matching Raycast error states. Wrap each pushed view in error boundary. Log errors to console with component stack. Create error_boundary.scss."

Files changed:
1. src/components/error_boundary/error_boundary.tsx (new)
2. src/components/error_boundary/error_boundary.scss (new)
3. src/components/icons/status_icons.tsx (added WarningIcon)
4. src/components/icons/index.ts (exported WarningIcon)
5. src/App.tsx (wrapped pushed views with ErrorBoundary)

Review criteria:
- Does the implementation fully satisfy the task spec?
- Are there any bugs, edge cases, or accessibility issues?
- Does the styling follow existing patterns (alert.scss as reference)?
- Is the ErrorBoundary integration in App.tsx correct (key-based reset, onReset to pop navigation)?
- Are there any TypeScript issues, workarounds, or `as any`?
- Does the component follow BEM naming, tokenized values, CSS variables for colors?
- Is the WarningIcon SVG well-formed?
- Any missing ARIA attributes?

Read all the changed files and provide a thorough review. Be specific about any issues found.

content.text:
Here is my thorough review of the INFRA006 ErrorBoundary implementation.

---

## Code Review: INFRA006 -- ErrorBoundary Component

### 1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/error_boundary/error_boundary.tsx`

**Issue 1 (Bug): `onKeyDown` handler will never fire.**
The outer `<div className="error-boundary">` has an `onKeyDown` handler for Escape, but the `div` is not focusable. It has no `tabIndex`. The `autoFocus` on the "Try Again" button means keypresses while that button is focused will bubble up to the div -- but only if the user hasn't tabbed away or clicked elsewhere. More critically, if the user clicks the "Show Details" toggle or the stack trace `<pre>`, focus leaves the button, and the div itself cannot receive keyboard events because it's not in the tab order. This is a real interaction gap.

**Fix:** Add `tabIndex={-1}` to the outer div so it can programmatically receive focus, or move the `onKeyDown` handler to a `useEffect`-based `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)` equivalent via `componentDidMount`/`componentWillUnmount`. Given this is a class component, the lifecycle approach is cleaner and guarantees Escape always works regardless of focus state.

**Issue 2 (Bug): `handleRetry` calls `onReset` before clearing error state.**
On line 36-40, `this.props.onReset?.()` (which is `nav.pop`) is called *before* `this.setState(...)`. In App.tsx, `onReset` is `nav.pop`, which changes the navigation state and could unmount this ErrorBoundary (since the `key` prop changes with `nav.navKey`). If the component unmounts before `setState` executes, React will warn about calling setState on an unmounted component. The order should be reversed: clear internal state first, then call `onReset`.

**Fix:**
```tsx
private handleRetry = () => {
  this.setState({ error: null, showDetails: false }, () => {
    this.props.onReset?.();
  });
};
```

**Issue 3 (Minor/Accessibility): Missing `aria-live` on the error container.**
The codebase uses `aria-live` in `hud.tsx`, `toast.tsx`, and `translator_view.tsx` for dynamic status announcements. The `role="alert"` on the outer div is good (it implicitly has `aria-live="assertive"`), so this is actually correct. However, there is no `aria-label` or `aria-describedby` linking the title/message for screen readers. This is not blocking but worth noting -- the `role="alert"` will announce the full text content, which is acceptable.

**Issue 4 (Spec compliance): Error message is not "collapsed by default, expandable" -- the message itself is always visible.**
The spec says: "error message (collapsed by default, expandable)." The current implementation shows `errorMessage` always visible (line 75) and only collapses the *stack trace*. Re-reading the spec, this is ambiguous -- it could mean the detailed error message/stack should be collapsed. The current behavior (showing a brief message + collapsible stack) is actually the more user-friendly interpretation and matches Raycast's pattern, so this is acceptable. Just flagging the ambiguity.

**Issue 5 (TypeScript): `getDerivedStateFromError` return type is `Partial<ErrorBoundaryState>`.**
This is technically correct but overly broad. The React type signature for `getDerivedStateFromError` expects the full or partial state. Returning `{ error }` is fine, but a more precise type would be `Pick<ErrorBoundaryState, 'error'>`. This is a nit, not a blocker.

### 2. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/error_boundary/error_boundary.scss`

**Issue 6 (Inconsistency): `.error-boundary__icon` uses hardcoded `width: 48px; height: 48px`.**
The alert component uses `$space-8` (32px) for the icon container. The error boundary uses a hardcoded `48px`. This should either use a token or at minimum match the alert's approach. There is no `$space-12` or `48px` token defined in `_tokens.scss`. If 48px is the intended design, a comment or a local variable would at least signal the intent, but per project rules, no comments. Consider using `$space-6 * 2` or adding a token.

**Fix:** Either use `$space-8` to match alert.scss, or define a calculation: `width: $space-6 * 2`.

**Issue 7 (Inconsistency): `.error-boundary__message` uses `color: var(--text-600)` but `.error-boundary__title` uses `color: var(--color-text-primary)`.**
This is consistent with `alert.scss` (`alert__title` uses `--color-text-primary`, `alert__message` uses `--text-600`). No issue here.

**Issue 8 (Missing): `.error-boundary__button` has no `flex: 1` unlike `.alert__button`.**
The alert's button has `flex: 1` to fill the container width. The error boundary button does not. This is likely intentional since it's a single centered button rather than a row of buttons. Acceptable.

**Issue 9 (Missing): `.error-boundary__button` focus-visible uses `outline-offset: -2px` matching alert, which is correct.**

**Issue 10 (Nit): `.error-boundary__stack` uses `word-break: break-all`.**
`break-all` breaks words at arbitrary points. For stack traces, `overflow-wrap: break-word` (or `word-break: break-word`) would be more appropriate since it only breaks at word boundaries when necessary. `break-all` can make file paths unreadable by splitting mid-word unnecessarily.

**Fix:** Change `word-break: break-all` to `overflow-wrap: break-word`.

### 3. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/icons/status_icons.tsx` -- WarningIcon

**Issue 11 (SVG geometry): The triangle path `fill` attribute is missing.**
The triangle path at line 54 uses `{...STROKE_DEFAULTS}`, which includes `fill: 'none'`. This means the warning triangle is only an outline with no fill. This is consistent with all other icons in `status_icons.tsx` (BatteryIcon's rect, WifiIcon's paths, etc. all use stroke-only). The exclamation mark components (line and dot) use stroke and fill respectively. The SVG is well-formed and consistent with the icon system.

**Issue 12 (SVG validation): Triangle geometry check.**
The path `M7.13 2.5a1 1 0 011.74 0l5.5 9.5A1 1 0 0113.5 13.5h-11a1 1 0 01-.87-1.5l5.5-9.5z` -- the arc commands look correct for creating rounded corners on the triangle. The exclamation line `M8 6v3.5` and dot `cx="8" cy="11.25" r="0.75"` are centered. Looks well-formed.

### 4. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/icons/index.ts`

The `WarningIcon` export is correctly added to the `status_icons` group, alphabetically ordered alongside `BatteryIcon`, `BoltIcon`, `CloudIcon`, `WifiIcon`. No issues.

### 5. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx`

**Issue 13 (Correctness): `key={nav.navKey}` for ErrorBoundary reset.**
Using `key` to remount the ErrorBoundary when navigation changes is the correct pattern. When `navKey` changes, React will unmount the old ErrorBoundary and mount a new one, which automatically clears the error state. This means the `onReset` callback (which calls `nav.pop`) would pop navigation AND the key change would cause a remount. This is fine -- the key-based reset is the primary mechanism, and `onReset` handles the explicit "Try Again" button case.

**Issue 14 (Correctness): Both `isFullView` and non-fullView branches wrap content in ErrorBoundary.**
This is correct per the spec ("Wrap each pushed view in error boundary"). The root view (`RootSearchView`) is NOT wrapped, which is appropriate since it's the fallback and crashing there would be unrecoverable anyway.

**Issue 15 (Edge case): What happens if `renderNavView` returns `null`?**
If `getCommand` returns undefined or the command has no component, `renderNavView` returns `null`. The ErrorBoundary will render `null` as its children -- this is fine, no crash, just a blank view. Not a bug, but worth noting there's no empty-state handling.

### Summary of Required Fixes

| # | Severity | File | Issue |
|---|----------|------|-------|
| 1 | **Bug** | error_boundary.tsx | `onKeyDown` for Escape will not work when focus is outside the button -- the div is not focusable |
| 2 | **Bug** | error_boundary.tsx | `onReset` called before `setState` risks setState-on-unmounted-component |
| 6 | **Medium** | error_boundary.scss | Hardcoded `48px` for icon container -- no token exists for this value |
| 10 | **Low** | error_boundary.scss | `word-break: break-all` should be `overflow-wrap: break-word` for stack traces |

### Verdict

Two real bugs (Issues 1 and 2), one token consistency violation (Issue 6), and one minor CSS improvement (Issue 10). The rest of the implementation is solid: BEM naming is correct, tokenized values are used throughout, the WarningIcon SVG is well-formed, the App.tsx integration with key-based reset is the right pattern, and no `as any` or TypeScript workarounds are present. The accessibility story is decent with `role="alert"`, `aria-expanded`, `type="button"`, and `focus-visible` handling -- but the Escape key bug undermines keyboard accessibility.

**Recommendation:** Fix Issues 1, 2, 6, and 10 before merging.

More critically, if the user clicks the "Show Details" toggle or the stack trace `<pre>`, focus leaves the button, and the div itself cannot receive keyboard events because it's not in the tab order.      webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).

     Placeholder color $text-tertiary. Create search_bar.tsx and
     search_bar.scss.

- [x] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  root search and all view search bars.
  Done: Recursive-descent fuzzy scorer with greedy fallback when recursion
  cap (10k calls) is reached. Scoring: consecutive match (+8), word boundary
  (+10), first char (+12), case match (+1), position bonus (1-idx/len)*2,
  exact match (+50), gap penalties (-3 start, -1 per). Word boundary detection
  handles separators, camelCase, and digit-letter transitions. HighlightedText
  component accepts optional pre-computed indices to avoid double scoring.
  Uses <span> (not <mark>) for accessibility. SCSS uses $font-weight-semibold
  token. command_registry.ts searchCommands() scores and sorts by relevance.
  All 4 view search bars (Clipboard, Snippets, Emoji, Files) use fuzzyMatch
  for filtering and pass query to ListItem for highlighting. App.tsx root
  search passes query to ListItem. All 93 tests pass, bun run check passes.
  Sub-tasks:
  - [x] SRCH001a - Create src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with fuzzyScore() and
    fuzzyMatch() functions. Score: consecutive match bonus, word boundary bonus,
    early position bonus, gap penalty. Return matched indices for highlighting.
  - [x] SRCH001b - Create HighlightedText component (src/components/highlighted_text/)
    that renders text with matched characters highlighted (bold). Takes text + query.
  - [x] SRCH001c - Update command_registry.ts searchCommands() to use fuzzy search
    with scoring and result sorting by score.
